program_chi: 性別研究社會科學學士
learning_outcomes:
1 1. Major Programme: On completion of the Programme, graduates should be able
to: Knowledge (i) develop knowledge of multidisciplinary theories and methodologies of
gender; (ii) communicate
effectively broad-based and in-depth understanding of local and
cross-cultural issues related to gender ideology, institutions, and
relationships; and (iii) articulate
critical understanding of debates and developments related to social justice,
equality, and peace at local and global levels. Skills (i) apply
skills in innovative and critical thinking as a member within a community of
scholars, as citizens and in the work place; (ii) employ
skills for social research analysis to a broad range of professional and
disciplinary contexts; (iii) integrate
theoretical analysis with empirical observation and lived experience, and to
link micro phenomena with macro systems within in complex, changing social
and cultural contexts; (iv) apply
problem-solving skills effectively to meet goals and challenges in the
workplace and personal life; (v) demonstrate
skills for effective communication, team work, and successful interpersonal
relationships with awareness and sensitivity to the diverse cultural
differences and backgrounds in order to address shared goals; and (vi) pursue
life-long learning. Values and Attitudes (i) develop
an open mind and readiness to embrace diversity; (ii) committed
to applying a sense of justice and fairness to daily life; (iii) appreciate
a broad set of humanist values, with a commitment to social harmony,
equality, and peace. 2. Minor Programme: (1) Sensitivity to the subtle
yet all-encompassing impact of sex and gender on human society. (2) Capability in integrating
perspectives from different disciplines and applying them to understanding
human lives and societies. (3) Capability in critiquing
and transcending prevalent social discourses. (4) Commitment to social
justice and equity.

Gender Studies Gender Studies Applicable to students admitted in 2023-24
Major Programme Requirement
(for Associate Degree or Higher Diploma holders admitted to senior-year
places)
Students are required to complete
a minimum of 54 units of courses as follows:

(a)  |  GDRS1001, 1002,
2010, 2011, 3005, 3007, 3009, 4012  |  24
(b) (i) (ii)  |  Choose one of the following
capstone courses: GDRS4009 GDRS4005 and 4010  |  3
2.  |  Elective Courses:  |  27
At least 27 units from the
following, with a maximum of 6 units of non-GDRS courses:
ANTH1310, 2310#, 2330#, 3360#,
3540#, COMM2920#, 3193#, CURE2014#, 2022#, 2036#, 2041#, 3021#, 3131#,
